factors.name: US equity market momentum
factors.summary: S&P 500 closed +1.17% near session highs, with Dow posting new record close per investingLive. Broader market finished above 100-hour MA at 7,420.76, indicating upward momentum into quarter-end.

factors.name: Supreme Court Fed independence ruling
factors.summary: Fed's Cook confirmed Supreme Court ruling 'defends central bank's independence,' reducing regulatory overhang. However, contradiction on FTC/Fed authority creating mixed long-term policy signal.

factors.name: US-Iran MOU geopolitical de-escalation
factors.summary: US-Iran agreement to halt attacks lifts geopolitical risk premium; oil prices rose to >$70/bbl, reducing stagflation fears. Talks scheduled for Tuesday in Doha provide near-term certainty.

factors.name: Dollar strength (DXY breakout)
factors.summary: USD broke above year-long trading range on Fed hawkishness signal; DXY near 101.10. Multinational Dow components (50% int'l revenue) face translation headwinds; yen at 40-year low vs USD creates JPY-hedging costs.

factors.name: Japan economic weakness
factors.summary: May industrial production +0.5% m/m vs +1.1% expected; y/y fell -1.7%. Japan lost World Cup match same session (USDJPY hit 161.97, 40-year high), signaling macro softness and potential BOJ pressure points for USD pair.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yield anchor
factors.summary: 10-year yields flat at 4.37% per investingLive wrap. No major catalyst in 24h window to move long-end; financials and dividend-heavy Dow names remain stable on rates.

factors.name: Tech mega-cap rally (Tesla +8%, AeroVironment +19%)
factors.summary: Tesla self-driving update drove +8% gain; AeroVironment earnings beat on $1.2B backlog. Nasdaq-heavy tech outperformance spilling into Dow via XLK exposure and mega-cap index composition.

factors.name: Oil price recovery post-Iran deal
factors.summary: WTI crude >$70/bbl after US-Iran MOU; energy input cost pressures ease. Reduces cyclical headwind for transport, manufacturing, and energy-intensive industrials in Dow.

factors.name: China AI underperformance and PMI data pending
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports China stocks trailing most since 2001 on AI boom fading. Official China PMI due Tuesday (June 30); Caterpillar, Boeing, materials demand all exposed. Mixed results expected per investingLive.

factors.name: Quarter-end position squaring (suspected)
factors.summary: investingLive notes market skepticism that 2% Nasdaq rally is 'quarter-end position squaring rather than fundamentals.' Suggests choppy, shallow consolidation risk into July 1st close.

factors.name: Fed policy credibility intact post-Supreme Court
factors.summary: Cook statement reassures Fed independence defended. Markets priced in no major policy shock; reduces tail-risk on rate surprise.

factors.name: Comcast NBCUniversal spinoff (M&A catalyst)
factors.summary: Comcast announced tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal and Sky. Dow has media/telecom exposure; spinoff is constructive for capital allocation but not immediate macro driver.

key_levels.support: 52100 (session low proxy, support from 100-hour MA confluence)
key_levels.resistance: 52300 (new record territory, watch for quarter-end profit-taking)

screening.narrative: Dow reached new record close on geopolitical relief (US-Iran de-escalation) and Fed independence reassurance post-Supreme Court ruling. Tech mega-caps (Tesla, AV) leading rally; strong finish near session highs suggests bullish posture into quarter-end. However, dollar strength (DXY breakout, yen 40-year low) creates multinational headwind, and Japan industrial production miss signals global growth softness. Move is already ~1% in 24h—typical for quarter-end squaring rather than fundamental repricing—creating PHASE 2 caution.
catalysts_ahead: China official PMI (June 30 early Asia) — critical for Caterpillar, Boeing demand signals
RBA minutes (June 30 Asia) — AUD-sensitive, potential hike signal could affect USD carry
US jobs data (early July) — NFP/jobless claims will reframe Fed rate cut expectations
FOMC decision (mid-July) — currently no near-term surprise priced; watch Powell presser
Dow component earnings season ramping (GS, JPM, BAC early July) — financial guidance on net interest margins vs lower yen BOJ support
screening.risk_warning: If China PMI comes in weaker than expected (sub-50 on manufacturing) or Japan announces emergency BOJ intervention to defend yen, dollar strength could morph into deflationary shock, triggering risk-off reversion. Alternatively, if US-Iran talks collapse Tuesday, geopolitical premium snaps back; oil spike >$80 would pressure cyclicals. Dow at record valuations with modest daily moves (0.10% 4h, 0.02% 24h) suggests thin conviction—watch for 1%+ pullback to retest 52100-52000 zone before resuming.

factors.name: China Manufacturing PMI (June)
factors.summary: China factory PMI beat at 50.3 vs 50.1 expected, driven by AI-linked export strength. Services PMI also beat at 50.2 vs 49.9 expected, supporting cyclical demand for Dow industrials (CAT, BA).

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Tensions & Diplomacy Signals
factors.summary: Conflicting signals on Doha talks create uncertainty. Trump says meeting 'perhaps important,' Iran denies talks planned. Oil volatility (WTI near $70, aluminum worst month since 2008) suggests market caution on supply disruptions and trade flows.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Prices
factors.summary: WTI retreated to ~$70 as Hormuz supply risks ease and Iran talk hopes temper demand. Lower oil inputs are positive for margins, but signals reduced geopolitical premium and softer demand outlook hurt energy-linked industrials (CVX).

factors.name: USD Strength & Yen Intervention Risk
factors.summary: USD/JPY hit 162.40 (40-year low for yen), DXY above 101.30. Stronger dollar supports Dow multinational earnings translation. However, Japan intervention threats create flash-crash risk and trade volatility.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: DXY strength and inflation concerns from Iran war keep some hawkish Fed bets in play, but oil price retreat and easing geopolitical pressures reduce urgency. ECB sources signal no rush to July move if June inflation surprise is mild.

factors.name: Gold Weakness & Real Yields
factors.summary: Gold dropped below $4,000 (YTD low ~$3,943) on USD strength and inflation concerns from Iran tensions offsetting safe-haven demand. Lower gold signals either rising real yields or reduced deflation fear—both headwinds for growth.

factors.name: Japan Industrial Production (May)
factors.summary: Japan May industrial production +0.5% m/m vs +1.1% expected; -1.7% y/y. Weak showing from key Asia manufacturing hub signals softer global demand, threatening Dow suppliers (BA, CAT).

factors.name: Credit & Corporate Sentiment
factors.summary: Warren Buffett skipping Gates Foundation donation; no broader credit stress signals in news. Dow heavyweights (JPM, GS) not flagged with earnings revisions yet; limited Q3 guidance visibility.

factors.name: Earnings & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings or guidance in this window. Q3 'shopping' headlines note investor positioning but lack catalysts. UNH, MSFT, BA, CAT guidance will be critical in coming weeks.

factors.name: Price Action Context (4h/24h Change)
factors.summary: +0.10% in 4h, +0.02% in 24h indicates minimal momentum. Prior bias was BUY6/10 with no confirmation—stalled setup suggests range-bound consolidation before directional break.

screening.narrative: US30 faces conflicting cross-currents: China PMI beat and USD strength favor cyclicals and multinational translation, but weak Japan manufacturing, oil retreat, Iran talk uncertainty, and stalled price action (near flat 24h) suggest cautious positioning into Q3. Geopolitical premium is fading as supply risks ease, reducing both inflation fears and risk-on appetite. No major US economic data released in this window—markets are in a holding pattern ahead of US JOLTS, next Fed signals, and July 4 holiday shutdown.
catalysts_ahead: US JOLTS Job Openings (June) — likely this week, labor market pressure indicator
US NFP (early July) — key inflation/Fed decision signal
FOMC minutes or Powell commentary — rate path clarity
Dow component earnings guidance (CAT, BA, JPM, UNH, MSFT) — Q3 cyclical outlook
US-Iran diplomacy outcome (Doha talks status) — oil/geopolitical risk repricing
Japan BOJ policy signals (Sato press conference ~0800 GMT) — yen intervention/rate-hike timing
ECB speakers on July rate decision — global rate differentials
screening.risk_warning: Stalled momentum (+0.02% 24h) suggests no confirmed directional bias yet. If Iran talks collapse or Hormuz tensions spike, oil could re-rally, pressuring real yields and favoring defensive names over cyclicals—flip to BEARISH. Conversely, if China stimulus accelerates or US jobs data surprises hot, cyclicals (CAT, BA, industrials) re-accelerate and drive Dow higher. Japan intervention (even rumored) could flash-crash risk assets. Confidence capped at 4 due to mixed signals and lack of fresh US macro catalyst.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing—current price action is flat and lacks conviction. Wait for either a sustained break above 52,350 resistance (bullish confirmation on China tailwinds) or a close below 51,950 support (bearish on geopolitical de-risking) before entering. Next 48–72h should clarify Q3 narrative.
